questions.question: What is the pipeline bubble?
options: Idle time at start and end of the pipeline: stages with no work until the first microbatch reaches them, and after the last microbatch leaves
questions.explanation: With M microbatches and N stages, per-stage bubble is (N-1)/(M+N-1). Picking M much greater than N shrinks it.

questions.question: Why is GPipe's activation memory proportional to M?
options: All M microbatches' activations must be held until the matching backward; nothing frees until the drain begins
questions.explanation: GPipe fills the pipeline with all forwards before any backward. Each in-flight microbatch carries its activations until its backward starts.

questions.question: How does 1F1B improve over GPipe?
options: Same bubble but bounded activation memory; once mb 0's forward reaches the last stage, its backward begins and activations free
questions.explanation: Megatron-LM and PipeDream use 1F1B to bound activation memory by pipeline depth, not microbatch count.

questions.question: Why is equal compute per stage more important than equal parameter count?
options: The slowest stage gates every cycle; other stages idle waiting for it. Embedding layers have many parameters but little compute, so partitioning by parameter count misbalances the pipeline
questions.explanation: Megatron-LM exposes --num-layers-per-stage as a list to allow uneven layer counts when per-layer cost differs.

questions.question: What goes wrong if every stage calls send before recv?
options: Deadlock on the wire; standard fix is to interleave even-rank send-first and odd-rank recv-first
questions.explanation: Synchronous send/recv pairs need one side to be receiving when the other sends. The lesson schedules ranks explicitly so the pattern is visible.

questions.question: Pipeline plus ZeRO-1 combines what two memory wins?
options: Per-stage parameter sharding (each rank only holds one stage of layers) plus per-rank optimiser sharding (each rank only holds 1/N of optimiser state for its stage)
questions.explanation: Pipeline shards layers across ranks; ZeRO shards optimiser state within the data-parallel group at each pipeline rank. Both wins compound.
